ground_truth: Prostate cancer–specific survival, Failure-free survival Progression-free survival Metastatic progression-free survival Symptomatic skeletal-related events toxic effects and adverse events
predicted: prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment.

ground_truth: 1. Standard of care + abiraterone acetate + prednisolone 2. Standard of care + abiraterone acetate + prednisolone + enzalutamide
predicted: Standard of care plus abiraterone; Standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide

The clinical trial provided several types of treatment: 1. Standard of care, which included androgen deprivation therapy (luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists or antagonists or bilateral orchiectomy) and, after December 17, 2015, docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles) with prednisolone (10 mg orally once per day). 2. Standard of care plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) and prednisolone (5 mg) orally once a day. 3. Standard of care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide (160 mg) orally once a day.

predicted: progression-free survival (PFS), prostate-specific antigen (PSA) level at 7 months (≤ 0.2 v0.2 to ≤ 4 v > 4 ng/mL), and adverse event profile.
